corpus.3ecc762c-6513-4a9a-930b-4b48db8ac431:
1 
 
Minutes of the Federal Open Market 
Committee 
September 17–18, 2024 
A joint meeting of the Federal Open Market Committee and the Board of Governors of the Federal
corpus.fe0f428b-b565-4856-bfc1-9f88cf1f632a:
Reserve System was held in the offices of the Board of Governors on Tuesday, September 17, 2024, 
at 10:30 a.m. and continued on Wednesday, September 18, 2024, at 9:00 a.m.1 
Developments in Financial Markets and Open Market Operations
corpus.b19ba83f-9225-47b2-be20-9f110408589f:
The manager turned first to a review of developments in financial markets.  Nominal Treasury yields 
declined notably over the period, driven by weaker-than-expected data releases—especially the July
corpus.63394636-0fb2-4f29-b8d5-bdb45098e5eb:
employment report in early August—and policy communications that were seen as affirming 
expectations that a reduction in policy restraint would begin at this meeting.  The decline in nominal
corpus.02dd926c-e8f9-49f1-9b2f-7e212876ca13:
yields over the period was primarily attributable to lower expected real yields, but measures of 
inflation compensation declined as well.  Broad equity prices finished the period modestly higher,
corpus.8a2783c7-b2b4-4a4a-a7f4-cd23e864ee2d:
while credit spreads had come off the very tight levels seen earlier this year but were still narrow by 
historical standards.  Overall, risky asset prices were compatible with continued economic expansion.
corpus.257139da-fef7-4922-977b-e28671d486f5:
The manager also discussed the brief episode of elevated market volatility in early August.  That 
episode saw some large moves in U.S. and foreign equity indexes, equity-implied volatilities, the
corpus.d533aa3a-a515-47cb-99bc-deafdbc9328f:
dollar–yen exchange rate, and Treasury yields.  These sharp moves appeared to be the result of a 
rapid unwinding of some speculative trading positions induced by unrelated events—such as the
corpus.3199ea90-57a0-41fb-8ee1-3ddc0f7018c2:
unexpectedly inflation-focused communications from the Bank of Japan (BOJ) in late July and the 
weaker-than-expected U.S. employment report in early August—and amplified by technical and liquidity
corpus.4f8cbb97-3bfa-45d6-8fa2-737daa0d496a:
factors.  All told, the unwinding process was contained, and market functioning recovered relatively 
quickly. 
Turning to policy expectations, the manager noted that the market-implied policy rate path shifted
corpus.1a875759-6136-4d72-a185-6b593611db88:
down materially.  At the time of the September meeting, the modal path for the federal funds rate 
implied by options prices was consistent with about 100 basis points of cuts through year-end,
corpus.1d2554c7-b2c2-406e-ab59-920f64e90bcf:
compared with around 50 basis points at the time of the July meeting.  The average path for the 
federal funds rate obtained from futures prices also shifted notably lower and remained below the
corpus.8fc49a38-9889-49fc-b3f8-fc72b2513dd8:
options-implied modal path, likely reflecting investors’ perception that risks were tilted toward more 
 
1 The Federal Open Market Committee is referenced as the “FOMC” and the “Committee” in these minutes; the Board of
corpus.b6a58fe5-9de9-40d1-bc84-ff70cad118a5: Governors of the Federal Reserve System is referenced as the “Board” in these minutes.
corpus.fa9fc750-39dc-4e9d-8385-29897d7ff7e0:
2    September 17–18, 2024 
 
rather than fewer cuts.  In the Open Market Desk’s Survey of Primary Dealers and Survey of Market 
Participants, most respondents had a modal expectation of a 25 basis point cut at this meeting,
corpus.a2e62879-5ec1-49e7-94c4-8294418edcb5:
though the manager also noted that, since the time of the surveys—about a week earlier—the 
probability of a 50 basis point cut at the September meeting implied by futures prices had increased
corpus.40724fff-1b8a-44eb-b29e-223b4e9693d9:
and exceeded the implied probability of a 25 basis point cut.  The median respondent’s modal path for 
the federal funds rate shifted down notably over the next two years, in line with the options-implied
corpus.0ff7025e-2323-4665-bf89-8abc63e2c150:
modal path, and was unchanged thereafter.  Balance sheet expectations in the surveys were little 
changed from July.  Most survey respondents did not appear to be concerned about an economic
corpus.c6358bc2-1262-455c-964a-a300639f4c26:
downturn in either the near or medium term; the median dealer’s most likely path of the 
unemployment rate for the next few years was only modestly higher than that in the July survey and 
was roughly stable around current levels.
corpus.1cbd5b20-b6d4-4084-a3cf-f1cc70ee9212:
In international developments, many central banks in advanced foreign economies (AFEs) had begun 
or continued to lower policy rates during the intermeeting period, with the Bank of England (BOE)
corpus.11cb20a1-40a4-458b-a016-26f4b75edd22:
deciding to initiate its rate-cutting cycle with a 25 basis point reduction and the European Central 
Bank (ECB) and the Bank of Canada (BOC) delivering their second and third 25 basis point cut,
corpus.ab982e7f-2e11-4f6c-b121-ac9b483888c2:
respectively.  The market-implied expectations for year-end policy rates fell over the period for most 
central banks in AFEs, although by a smaller amount than they did for the Federal Reserve,
corpus.f321c1ca-1f7b-4919-8bf1-ed049c6e3f77:
contributing to a modest decline in the trade-weighted U.S. dollar index. 
The manager then turned to money markets and Desk operations.  Unsecured overnight rates
corpus.996c8102-f602-4d84-b207-0102a455ef5e:
remained stable over the intermeeting period.  In secured funding markets, rates on overnight 
repurchase agreements (repo) were higher than a few months earlier amid large issuance of Treasury
corpus.b95275a9-4817-4e53-bd81-0e8d1104f89a:
securities and elevated demand for securities financing but were little changed, on net, over the 
period.  The manager discussed the interconnections between the repo and federal funds markets,
corpus.578858b7-2a39-400f-a518-ccbff277a477:
underscoring the importance of monitoring a range of indicators to assess reserve conditions and the 
state of money markets.  Looking at a range of such indicators, the manager concluded that reserves 
appeared to remain abundant.
corpus.59df5c35-5283-466d-9a44-6c6fb88b3bce:
Usage of the overnight reverse repurchase agreement (ON RRP) facility declined about $100 billion 
over the intermeeting period, helped by an increase in the net supply of Treasury bills.  With net bill
corpus.8ccf7af1-d1a6-49a0-924c-446c1fb5dce5:
supply expected to decrease as a result of the September tax date before increasing again, the staff 
assessed that the decline in ON RRP usage might slow over the coming intermeeting period before
corpus.88f553a6-1839-4cad-9a13-228e73fe50a7:
resuming later this year.  The manager also added that, with the concentration of ON RRP usage in a 
small number of fund complexes, there was an increased risk that idiosyncratic allocation decisions
corpus.91647487-9a77-4cf5-93cc-b1a45e9392d3: could have an outsized effect on aggregate ON RRP volumes.
corpus.abcdf575-ce89-4177-a294-4b37d3245054:
Minutes of the Federal Open Market Committee    3 
 
By unanimous vote, the Committee ratified the Desk’s domestic transactions over the intermeeting
corpus.6311d9f5-d5f3-4ae5-8c8d-d464590766cd:
period.  There were no intervention operations in foreign currencies for the System’s account during 
the intermeeting period. 
Staff Review of the Economic Situation
corpus.c68e6d87-04f6-4739-9f59-eb89826d9cb4:
The information available at the time of the meeting indicated that real gross domestic product (GDP) 
had expanded solidly so far this year.  The pace of job gains continued to moderate since the
corpus.598f083f-edfe-4a95-8395-3e0caff268a4:
beginning of the year, and the unemployment rate had moved up but remained low.  Consumer price 
inflation was well below its year-earlier rate but remained somewhat elevated.
corpus.a58817a6-7164-4e6d-aaeb-c023d33c6e3e:
Consumer price inflation—as measured by the 12-month change in the price index for personal 
consumption expenditures (PCE)—was lower in July than it had been in March, which had followed
corpus.b3fa899e-1ae9-475f-941b-ef96f2ae01a3:
some high month-over-month changes in the beginning of the year.  Monthly changes in PCE prices 
since April had been smaller than those seen in the first three months of the year.  On a 12-month
corpus.a9940387-3e86-421e-9bd9-bd4e721469d8:
basis, total PCE price inflation was 2.5 percent in July, and core PCE price inflation—which excludes 
changes in energy prices and many consumer food prices—was 2.6 percent.  In August, the 12-month
corpus.b7d2e0a6-3547-4e60-a1dd-c3b8c35b748f:
change in the consumer price index (CPI) was 2.5 percent, and core CPI inflation was 3.2 percent; 
both measures were well below their rates from a year ago.  The staff estimated, given both the CPI
corpus.b2f73e40-ebff-4e86-a0f7-ef9265006330:
and producer price index data, that total PCE price inflation would be 2.2 percent over the 12 months 
ending in August and that core PCE price inflation would be 2.7 percent.
corpus.0efaccec-b31c-44ad-b397-e783cdc858fc:
Recent data suggested that labor market conditions had eased further but remained solid.  Over July 
and August, average monthly nonfarm payroll gains were less than their average second-quarter pace,
corpus.c4610242-d1d8-4217-977c-a83937f7d720:
the unemployment rate edged up to 4.2 percent, the labor force participation rate ticked up, and the 
employment-to-population ratio ticked down.  The unemployment rate for African Americans moved
corpus.b077ed5b-e18a-41de-a6d4-5b423240f1aa:
down, while the rate for Hispanics rose, and both rates were above those for Asians and for Whites.  
The ratio of job vacancies to unemployment edged down to 1.1 in August, a bit below its level just
corpus.371bb092-a74c-4970-aa34-2da9cb9c10f5:
before the pandemic.  Job layoffs, as measured by initial claims for unemployment insurance benefits, 
remained low through August.  Measures of nominal labor compensation continued to decelerate.
corpus.67e46c73-a62e-42b3-b54d-3580eb5d6dcd:
Average hourly earnings for all employees rose 3.8 percent over the 12 months ending in August, and 
the four-quarter change in business-sector compensation per hour was 3.1 percent in the second
corpus.686b054b-2909-4148-92e5-dc7712272706:
quarter.  Both measures were well below their pace from a year earlier.  
Real GDP rose solidly, on balance, over the first half of the year.  Real private domestic final purchases
corpus.3b943c67-1fa2-4001-bc73-e083ac7654da:
(PDFP)—which comprises PCE and private fixed investment and which often provides a better signal 
than GDP of underlying economic momentum—posted a stronger first-half increase than GDP, and
corpus.81913d6b-e35b-4cba-bbab-564bdd263609: PDFP growth over the first half was only moderately slower than last year.  Recent indicators for third-
corpus.bbc770d1-1688-49c7-850f-4b39b0c3d128:
4    September 17–18, 2024 
 
quarter GDP and PDFP suggested that economic growth was continuing at a solid pace, particularly for 
PCE and business investment in equipment and intangibles.
corpus.a58bc6c0-e637-41d2-aa8f-20e3807bb324:
